{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | Image = | Image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.
{{Sabae Daimyō|5th|1786–1812}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | Image = | Image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.
{{Sabae Daimyō|5th|1786–1812}}

{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | Image = | Image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.
{{Sabae Daimyō|5th|1786–1812}}

{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | Image = | Image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} }}

{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | Image = | Image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.
{{Sabae Daimyō|5th|1786–1812}}

{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | Image = | Image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.
{{Sabae Daimyō|5th|1786–1812}}

{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | Image = | Image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.

{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.

{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.
{{Sabae Daimyō|5th|1786–1812}}

In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.
{{Sabae Daimyō|5th|1786–1812}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.
{{Sabae Daimyō|5th|1786–1812}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.
{{Sabae Daimyō|5th|1786–1812}}

{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.

'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. {{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.

{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.
{{Sabae Daimyō|5th|1786–1812}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.
{{Sabae Daimyō|5th|1786–1812}}

{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.
{{Sabae Daimyō|5th|1786–1812}}

{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.
{{Sabae Daimyō|5th|1786–1812}}

Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明|12=draft}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明|12=draft}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代|12=draft}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|12=draft}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学|12=draft}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明|12=draft}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|12=draft}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|12=draft}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化|12=draft}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=draft}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|12=draft}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市|12=draft}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=draft}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長|12=draft}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=draft}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|12=draft}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝|12=draft}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|12=draft}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|12=draft}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.
{{Sabae Daimyō|5th|1786–1812}}

Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.

{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.

revision.text:
{{統合文字|詮}} {{基礎情報 武士 | 氏名 = 間部詮煕 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[江戸時代]]中期 - 後期 | 生誕 = [[明和]]7年[[6月20日 (旧暦)|6月20日]]（[[1770年]][[7月12日]]） | 死没 = [[文化 (元号)|文化]]8年[[12月21日 (旧暦)|12月21日]]（[[1812年]][[2月3日]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = 内蔵、主膳（[[仮名 (通称)|通称]]） | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 霊名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[従五位|従五位下]]主膳正・[[若狭国|若狭]]守 | 幕府 = | 主君 = | 藩 = [[越前国|越前]][[鯖江藩]]主 | 氏族 = [[間部氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[間部詮茂]]、母：水谷氏の娘 | 兄弟 = '''詮煕'''、輝（[[間部詮芳]]室）、潔、[[山名義蕃]]、[[仙石久徳]]、豊、[[酒井忠固]] | 妻 = '''端冠院'''（[[牧野貞長]]の娘） | 子 = '''[[間部詮允|詮允]]'''、峯（[[水野忠篤 (鶴牧藩嫡子)|水野忠篤]]継室）、[[間部詮苞]]、敬（[[中西主水]]養女）、慶（[[米倉昌寿]]正室）、千重、弥、文、[[間部詮勝|詮勝]] | 特記事項 = }} '''間部 詮熙'''（まなべ あきひろ）は、[[越前国|越前]][[鯖江藩]]の第5代藩主。間部家6代。第4代藩主・[[間部詮茂]]の長男。母は家臣・水谷氏の娘。正室は[[牧野貞長]]の娘・端冠院。[[仮名 (通称)|通称]]は内蔵、主膳。
== 生涯 == [[天明]]5年（[[1785年]]）12月11日、[[征夷大将軍|将軍]]・[[徳川家治]]に[[御目見]]する。同年12月18日、[[従五位|従五位下]]・主膳正に叙任する。天明6年（[[1786年]]）7月晦日、詮茂の死去により跡を継ぐ。天明7年（[[1787年]]）6月22日、初めて帰国する許可を得る。
天明6年（1786年）9月23日、[[徳川家治]]の法事の際に[[上野]]・[[寛永寺]]の黒門勤番を務め、[[寛政]]10年（[[1798年]]）には公家御馳走人を務めた。藩政においては凶作が相次ぎ、しかも[[参勤交代]]や幕命による公役負担の増加で出費が増大し、それによって藩財政が窮乏化した。このため、厳しい倹約令を出し、さらに借金をローン制にし、[[上米]]制度も行なっている。
詮煕は[[儒学]]を奨励した。天明8年（[[1788年]]）に[[京都]]から[[芥川元澄]]（思堂）を藩儒として招き、藩士に儒学を講じさせ、後の藩校・[[進徳館 (鯖江藩)|進徳館]]創設のもとになった。元澄は後に[[間部氏|間部家]]の鯖江における歴史をまとめた『越前鯖江誌』を編纂している。詮熙自身は騎射に優れた才を見せたと言われている。
[[文化 (元号)|文化]]8年12月21日（[[1812年]]2月3日）、病没。享年42歳。没後、家督は長男の[[間部詮允|詮允]]が継いだ。法号は真霊院。墓所は[[福井県]][[鯖江市]]深江町の万慶寺。
== 系譜 == '''父母''' * [[間部詮茂]]（父） * 水谷氏 - 側室（母）
'''正室''' * 端冠院 - [[牧野貞長]]の娘
'''側室''' * 近藤氏 * 永田氏
'''子女''' * [[間部詮允]]（長男） 生母は近藤氏（側室） * [[間部詮苞]] * [[間部詮勝]]（五男） 生母は永田氏（側室） * 峯 - [[水野忠篤 (鶴牧藩嫡子)|水野忠篤]]継室 * 敬 - [[中西主水]]の養女 * 慶 - [[米倉昌寿]]正室 * 千重 * 弥 * 文
== 参考文献 == *間部家文書刊行会 編『間部家文書』第三巻 鯖江市、1984年。
{{鯖江藩主|5代|1786年 - 1812年}}
{{デフォルトソート:まなへ あきひろ}} [[Category:鯖江藩主|*05]] [[Category:鯖江間部家|あきひろ]] [[Category:1770年生]] [[Category:1812年没]]

[[sn:Manabe Akihiro]] [[ja:間部詮熙]] [[en:Manabe Akihiro]]

{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.
{{Sabae Daimyō|5th|1786–1812}}
