revision.text:
{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiwa|qq=}} 7, {{ill|6th month, 20th day|ja|6月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th month, 20th day|qq=}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka (era)|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka|qq=}} 8, {{ill|12th month, 21st day|ja|12月21日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/12th month, 21st day|qq=}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe clan|qq=}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|ja|牧野貞長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|qq=}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|qq=}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|qq=}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|qq=}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akikatsu|qq=}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō|qq=}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|ja|鯖江藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabae Domain|qq=}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Province|qq=}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei|qq=}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgun|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|ja|徳川家治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Ieharu|qq=}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei|qq=}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei|qq=}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|ja|参勤交代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankin-kōtai|qq=}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|qq=}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Confucianism|qq=}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei|qq=}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|qq=}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|qq=}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化 (元号)|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka|qq=}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|qq=}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabae|qq=}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|ja|牧野貞長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Sadanaga|qq=}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|qq=}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|ja|間部詮勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akikatsu|qq=}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|qq=}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|qq=}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.
{{Sabae Daimyō|5th|1786–1812}}

He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|qq=}} in {{ill|Sabae|ja|鯖江市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabae|qq=}}, Fukui Prefecture.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 7, [[:en:June 20|6th month, 20th day]] (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8, [[:en:December 21|12th month, 21st day]] (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe clan|qq=}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)]] | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|qq=}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|qq=}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|qq=}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō|qq=}} of the [[:en:Sabae Domain|Sabae Domain]] in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], [[:en:Tokugawa Ieharu|Tokugawa Ieharu]]. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to [[:en:Sankin-kōtai|Sankin-kōtai]] and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|qq=}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Confucianism|qq=}} within his domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|qq=}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|qq=}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|qq=}} in [[:en:Sabae, Fukui|Sabae]], Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga]])
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|qq=}} * [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|qq=}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|qq=}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.

{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 7, [[:en:June 20|6th month, 20th day]] (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8, [[:en:December 21|12th month, 21st day]] (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe clan|qq=}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)]] | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|qq=}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|qq=}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|qq=}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō|qq=}} of the [[:en:Sabae Domain|Sabae Domain]] in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], [[:en:Tokugawa Ieharu|Tokugawa Ieharu]]. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to [[:en:Sankin-kōtai|Sankin-kōtai]] and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|qq=}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|ja|儒学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Confucianism|qq=}} within his domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|qq=}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|qq=}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|qq=}} in [[:en:Sabae, Fukui|Sabae]], Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga]])
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|qq=}} * [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|qq=}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|qq=}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.

In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to [[:en:Sankin-kōtai|Sankin-kōtai]] and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged [[:en:Confucianism|Confucianism]] within his domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|qq=}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺}} in [[:en:Sabae, Fukui|Sabae]], Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga]])
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|qq=}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.

{{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 7, [[:en:June 20|6th month, 20th day]] (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8, [[:en:December 21|12th month, 21st day]] (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe clan|qq=}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)]] | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|qq=}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩}} of the [[:en:Sabae Domain|Sabae Domain]] in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], [[:en:Tokugawa Ieharu|Tokugawa Ieharu]]. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to [[:en:Sankin-kōtai|Sankin-kōtai]] and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged [[:en:Confucianism|Confucianism]] within his domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|qq=}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺}} in [[:en:Sabae, Fukui|Sabae]], Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga]])
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|qq=}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.

{{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1770|7|12}}|death_date={{death date|1812|2|3}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|qid=Q3285148}}; {{ill|Manabe Akisane|qid=Q11655468}}|father={{ill|Manabe Akitō|qid=Q11655473}}|relatives={{ill|Yamana Yoshiakira|qid=Q11467620}}|family={{ill|Manabe clan|qid=Q11655466}}|native_name=間部 詮熙|given_name={{ill|Akihiro|qid=Q4700926}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11655472]] Wikidata name: Manabe Akihiro [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 7, [[:en:June 20|6th month, 20th day]] (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8, [[:en:December 21|12th month, 21st day]] (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe clan|qq=}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)]] | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|qq=}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩}} of the [[:en:Sabae Domain|Sabae Domain]] in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], [[:en:Tokugawa Ieharu|Tokugawa Ieharu]]. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to [[:en:Sankin-kōtai|Sankin-kōtai]] and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged [[:en:Confucianism|Confucianism]] within his domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|qq=}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺}} in [[:en:Sabae, Fukui|Sabae]], Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga]])
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|qq=}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.

His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺|1=Bankei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620477}} in [[:en:Sabae, Fukui|Sabae]], Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga]])
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|qq=|1=Mizuno Tadamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi

{{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1770|7|12}}|death_date={{death date|1812|2|3}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|qid=Q3285148}}; {{ill|Manabe Akisane|qid=Q11655468}}|father={{ill|Manabe Akitō|qid=Q11655473}}|relatives={{ill|Yamana Yoshiakira|qid=Q11467620}}|family={{ill|Manabe clan|qid=Q11655466}}|native_name=間部 詮熙|given_name={{ill|Akihiro|qid=Q4700926}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11655472]] Wikidata name: Manabe Akihiro [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 7, [[:en:June 20|6th month, 20th day]] (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8, [[:en:December 21|12th month, 21st day]] (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe clan|qq=}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)]] | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|qq=|1=Mizuno Tadamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of the [[:en:Sabae Domain|Sabae Domain]] in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], [[:en:Tokugawa Ieharu|Tokugawa Ieharu]]. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to [[:en:Sankin-kōtai|Sankin-kōtai]] and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shogunate|WD=Q1074150}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged [[:en:Confucianism|Confucianism]] within his domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|qq=|1=Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20037568}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺|1=Bankei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620477}} in [[:en:Sabae, Fukui|Sabae]], Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|qq=|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga]])
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|qq=|1=Mizuno Tadamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.

His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], [[:en:Tokugawa Ieharu|Tokugawa Ieharu]]. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to [[:en:Sankin-kōtai|Sankin-kōtai]] and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shogunate|WD=Q1074150}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged [[:en:Confucianism|Confucianism]] within his domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|qq=|1=Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20037568}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺|1=Bankei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620477}} in [[:en:Sabae, Fukui|Sabae]], Fukui Prefecture.

'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga]])
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|qq=|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|qq=|1=Mizuno Tadamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi

{{ill|Manabe Akisane|WD=Q11655468}}|father={{ill|Manabe Akitō|WD=Q11655473}}|relatives={{ill|Yamana Yoshiakira|WD=Q11467620}}|family={{ill|Manabe clan|WD=Q11655466}}|native_name=間部 詮熙|given_name={{ill|Akihiro|WD=Q4700926}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11655472]] Wikidata name: Manabe Akihiro [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 7, [[:en:June 20|6th month, 20th day]] (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8, [[:en:December 21|12th month, 21st day]] (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe clan|qq=}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)]] | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|1=Mizuno Tadamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of the [[:en:Sabae Domain|Sabae Domain]] in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], [[:en:Tokugawa Ieharu|Tokugawa Ieharu]]. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to [[:en:Sankin-kōtai|Sankin-kōtai]] and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shogunate|WD=Q1074150}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged [[:en:Confucianism|Confucianism]] within his domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|1=Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20037568}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺|1=Bankei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620477}} in [[:en:Sabae, Fukui|Sabae]], Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga]])
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|1=Mizuno Tadamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:間部詮熙]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1770|7|12}}|death_date={{death date|1812|2|3}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148}}; {{ill|Manabe Akisane|WD=Q11655468}}|father={{ill|Manabe Akitō|WD=Q11655473}}|relatives={{ill|Yamana Yoshiakira|WD=Q11467620}}|family={{ill|Manabe clan|WD=Q11655466}}|native_name=間部 詮熙|given_name={{ill|Akihiro|WD=Q4700926}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11655472]] Wikidata name: Manabe Akihiro [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 7, [[:en:June 20|6th month, 20th day]] (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8, [[:en:December 21|12th month, 21st day]] (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe clan|qq=}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)]] | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|1=Mizuno Tadamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of the [[:en:Sabae Domain|Sabae Domain]] in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], [[:en:Tokugawa Ieharu|Tokugawa Ieharu]]. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to [[:en:Sankin-kōtai|Sankin-kōtai]] and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shogunate|WD=Q1074150}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged [[:en:Confucianism|Confucianism]] within his domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|1=Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20037568}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺|1=Bankei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620477}} in [[:en:Sabae, Fukui|Sabae]], Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga]])
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|1=Mizuno Tadamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:間部詮熙]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1770|7|12}}|death_date={{death date|1812|2|3}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148}}; {{ill|Manabe Akisane|WD=Q11655468}}|father={{ill|Manabe Akitō|WD=Q11655473}}|relatives={{ill|Yamana Yoshiakira|WD=Q11467620}}|family={{ill|Manabe clan|WD=Q11655466}}|native_name=間部 詮熙|given_name={{ill|Akihiro|WD=Q4700926}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11655472]] Wikidata name: Manabe Akihiro [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 7, [[:en:June 20|6th month, 20th day]] (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8, [[:en:December 21|12th month, 21st day]] (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe clan|qq=}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)]] | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|1=Mizuno Tadamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of the [[:en:Sabae Domain|Sabae Domain]] in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], [[:en:Tokugawa Ieharu|Tokugawa Ieharu]]. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to [[:en:Sankin-kōtai|Sankin-kōtai]] and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shogunate|WD=Q1074150}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged [[:en:Confucianism|Confucianism]] within his domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|1=Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20037568}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺|1=Bankei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620477}} in [[:en:Sabae, Fukui|Sabae]], Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga]])
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|1=Mizuno Tadamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.

His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺|1=Bankei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620477}} in [[:en:Sabae, Fukui|Sabae]], Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)

'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:間部詮熙]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:間部詮熙]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1770|7|12}}|death_date={{death date|1812|2|3}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148}}; {{ill|Manabe Akisane|WD=Q11655468}}|father={{ill|Manabe Akitō|WD=Q11655473}}|relatives={{ill|Yamana Yoshiakira|WD=Q11467620}}|family={{ill|Manabe clan|WD=Q11655466}}|native_name=間部 詮熙|given_name={{ill|Akihiro|WD=Q4700926}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11655472]] Wikidata name: Manabe Akihiro [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 7, [[:en:June 20|6th month, 20th day]] (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8, [[:en:December 21|12th month, 21st day]] (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe clan|qq=}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)]] | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|1=Mizuno Tadamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of the [[:en:Sabae Domain|Sabae Domain]] in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], [[:en:Tokugawa Ieharu|Tokugawa Ieharu]]. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to [[:en:Sankin-kōtai|Sankin-kōtai]] and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shogunate|WD=Q1074150}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged [[:en:Confucianism|Confucianism]] within his domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|1=Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20037568}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺|1=Bankei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620477}} in [[:en:Sabae, Fukui|Sabae]], Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga]])
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|1=Mizuno Tadamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi

In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|1=Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20037568}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺|1=Bankei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620477}} in [[:en:Sabae, Fukui|Sabae]], Fukui Prefecture.

'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of the [[:en:Sabae Domain|Sabae Domain]] in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], [[:en:Tokugawa Ieharu|Tokugawa Ieharu]]. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to [[:en:Sankin-kōtai|Sankin-kōtai]] and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shogunate|WD=Q1074150}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged [[:en:Confucianism|Confucianism]] within his domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|1=Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20037568}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺|1=Bankei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620477}} in [[:en:Sabae, Fukui|Sabae]], Fukui Prefecture.

In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to [[:en:Sankin-kōtai|Sankin-kōtai]] and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shogunate|WD=Q1074150}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged [[:en:Confucianism|Confucianism]] within his domain. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|1=Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20037568}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺|1=Bankei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620477}} in [[:en:Sabae, Fukui|Sabae]], Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of [[:en:Makino Sadanaga|Makino Sadanaga]])
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Manabe Akikatsu|Manabe Akikatsu]] (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|1=Mizuno Tadamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.
{{Sabae Daimyō|5th|1786–1812}}

'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|en|Sabae Domain|WD=Q3034517|lt=Sabae Domain}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shōgun}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|en|Tokugawa Ieharu|WD=Q437518|lt=Tokugawa Ieharu}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|en|Sankin-kōtai|WD=Q1151844|lt=Sankin-kōtai}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shogunate|WD=Q1074150}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|en|Confucianism|WD=Q9581|lt=Confucianism}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|1=Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20037568}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|WD=Q11042|lt=Bunka}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺|1=Bankei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620477}} in {{ill|Sabae, Fukui|en|Sabae, Fukui|WD=Q847598|lt=Sabae}}, Fukui Prefecture.
