{{ill|Manabe Akisane|WD=Q11655468}}|father={{ill|Manabe Akitō|WD=Q11655473}}|relatives={{ill|Yamana Yoshiakira|WD=Q11467620}}|family={{ill|Manabe clan|WD=Q11655466}}|native_name=間部 詮熙|given_name={{ill|Akihiro|WD=Q4700926}}}} {{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 7, {{ill|June 20|en|June 20|WD=Q2666|lt=6th month, 20th day}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|WD=Q11042|lt=Bunka}} 8, {{ill|December 21|en|December 21|WD=Q2603|lt=12th month, 21st day}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe clan|qq=}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|en|Makino Sadanaga|WD=Q6739705|lt=Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|1=Mizuno Tadamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|en|Sabae Domain|WD=Q3034517|lt=Sabae Domain}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shōgun}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|en|Tokugawa Ieharu|WD=Q437518|lt=Tokugawa Ieharu}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|en|Sankin-kōtai|WD=Q1151844|lt=Sankin-kōtai}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shogunate|WD=Q1074150}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|en|Confucianism|WD=Q9581|lt=Confucianism}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|1=Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20037568}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|WD=Q11042|lt=Bunka}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺|1=Bankei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620477}} in {{ill|Sabae, Fukui|en|Sabae, Fukui|WD=Q847598|lt=Sabae}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|en|Makino Sadanaga|WD=Q6739705|lt=Makino Sadanaga}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|1=Mizuno Tadamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.
{{Sabae Daimyō|5th|1786–1812}}

He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺|1=Bankei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620477}} in {{ill|Sabae, Fukui|en|Sabae, Fukui|WD=Q847598|lt=Sabae}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|en|Makino Sadanaga|WD=Q6739705|lt=Makino Sadanaga}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|1=Mizuno Tadamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:間部詮熙]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:間部詮熙]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:間部詮熙]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1770|7|12}}|death_date={{death date|1812|2|3}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|1=Manabe Akikatsu}}; {{ill|Manabe Akisane|WD=Q11655468|1=Manabe Akisane}}|father={{ill|Manabe Akitō|WD=Q11655473|1=Manabe Akitō}}|relatives={{ill|Yamana Yoshiakira|WD=Q11467620|1=Yamana Yoshiakira}}|family={{ill|Manabe clan|WD=Q11655466|1=Manabe clan}}|native_name=間部 詮熙|given_name={{ill|Akihiro|WD=Q4700926|1=Akihiro}}}} {{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa|1=Meiwa}} 7, {{ill|June 20|en|June 20|WD=Q2666|lt=6th month, 20th day|1=June 20}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|WD=Q11042|lt=Bunka|1=Culture}} 8, {{ill|December 21|en|December 21|WD=Q2603|lt=12th month, 21st day|1=December 21}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe clan|qq=}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|en|Makino Sadanaga|WD=Q6739705|lt=Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|1=Makino Sadanaga}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|1=Q11549383|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu|1=Manabe Akikatsu}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=Han system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|en|Sabae Domain|WD=Q3034517|lt=Sabae Domain|1=Sabae Domain}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province|1=Echizen Province}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shōgun|1=Shogun}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|en|Tokugawa Ieharu|WD=Q437518|lt=Tokugawa Ieharu|1=Tokugawa Ieharu}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|en|Sankin-kōtai|WD=Q1151844|lt=Sankin-kōtai|1=Sankin-kōtai}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shogunate|WD=Q1074150}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|en|Confucianism|WD=Q9581|lt=Confucianism|1=Confucianism}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|1=Q20037568|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20037568}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|WD=Q11042|lt=Bunka|1=Culture}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺|1=Bankei-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620477}} in {{ill|Sabae, Fukui|en|Sabae, Fukui|WD=Q847598|lt=Sabae|1=Sabae, Fukui}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|en|Makino Sadanaga|WD=Q6739705|lt=Makino Sadanaga|1=Makino Sadanaga}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu|1=Manabe Akikatsu}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|1=Q11549383|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.

{{ill|Manabe Akisane|WD=Q11655468|1=Manabe Akisane}}|father={{ill|Manabe Akitō|WD=Q11655473|1=Manabe Akitō}}|relatives={{ill|Yamana Yoshiakira|WD=Q11467620|1=Yamana Yoshiakira}}|family={{ill|Manabe clan|WD=Q11655466|1=Manabe clan}}|native_name=間部 詮熙|given_name={{ill|Akihiro|WD=Q4700926|1=Akihiro}}}} {{Infobox Samurai | Name = Manabe Akihiro | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} (Middle to Late) | Birth Date = {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa|1=Meiwa}} 7, {{ill|June 20|en|June 20|WD=Q2666|lt=6th month, 20th day|1=June 20}} (July 12, 1770) | Death Date = {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|WD=Q11042|lt=Bunka|1=Culture}} 8, {{ill|December 21|en|December 21|WD=Q2603|lt=12th month, 21st day|1=December 21}} (February 3, 1812) | Other Names = Kurō, Shūzen (common names) | Posthumous Name = | Clan = {{ill|Manabe clan|ja|間部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe clan|qq=}} | Father = {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} | Mother = Daughter of the Mizutani clan | Spouse = {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|en|Makino Sadanaga|WD=Q6739705|lt=Makino Sadanaga's daughter (Tankan-in)|1=Makino Sadanaga}} | Children = {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son), Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|1=Q11549383|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}), {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kei (adopted by {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), Chie, Ya, Fumi, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu|1=Manabe Akikatsu}} }}
'''Manabe Akihiro''' (間部 詮煕) was the fifth {{ill|Daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=Han system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of the {{ill|Sabae Domain|en|Sabae Domain|WD=Q3034517|lt=Sabae Domain|1=Sabae Domain}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province|1=Echizen Province}}, and the sixth head of the Manabe family. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}}. His mother was a concubine from the Mizutani family. His common names were Kurō and Shūzen.
== Life == In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 5 (1785), on the 11th day of the 12th month, Akihiro had his first audience with the {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shōgun|1=Shogun}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieharu|en|Tokugawa Ieharu|WD=Q437518|lt=Tokugawa Ieharu|1=Tokugawa Ieharu}}. On the 18th day of the same month, he was appointed {{ill|Jūgoi|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and given the title Shūzen-no-shō (主膳正). Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|en|Sankin-kōtai|WD=Q1151844|lt=Sankin-kōtai|1=Sankin-kōtai}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shogunate|WD=Q1074150}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|en|Confucianism|WD=Q9581|lt=Confucianism|1=Confucianism}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|1=Q20037568|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20037568}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|WD=Q11042|lt=Bunka|1=Culture}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺|1=Bankei-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620477}} in {{ill|Sabae, Fukui|en|Sabae, Fukui|WD=Q847598|lt=Sabae|1=Sabae, Fukui}}, Fukui Prefecture.
== Family == '''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Manabe Akishige|ja|間部詮茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akishige|1=Manabe Akitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655473}} * Mother: Mizutani family (concubine)
'''Wife:''' * Tankan-in (daughter of {{ill|Makino Sadanaga|en|Makino Sadanaga|WD=Q6739705|lt=Makino Sadanaga|1=Makino Sadanaga}})
'''Concubines:''' * Kondō family * Nagata family
'''Children:''' * {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655468}} (eldest son, by Kondō) * {{ill|Manabe Akitomo|ja|間部詮苞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akitomo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu|1=Manabe Akikatsu}} (fifth son, by Nagata) * Mine (wife of {{ill|Mizuno Tadamichi|ja|水野忠篤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadamichi|1=Q11549383|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549383}}) * Kei (adopted daughter of {{ill|Nakanishi Shun|ja|中西主水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Shun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Chie * Ya * Fumi
== References == * Manabe-ke Monjo Kankōkai (間部家文書刊行会), ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 3, Sabae City, 1984.
{{Sabae Daimyō|5th|1786–1812}}

Following the death of his father Akishige in {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|qid=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 6 (1786), Akihiro succeeded as head of the domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|qid=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 7 (1787), he received permission to return to Sabae for the first time.
During his tenure, Akihiro managed domain affairs during a time of consecutive poor harvests and increased expenditures due to {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|en|Sankin-kōtai|qid=Q1151844|lt=Sankin-kōtai|1=Sankin-kōtai}} and public duties imposed by the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shogunate|qid=Q1074150}}. To address financial difficulties, he implemented strict austerity measures, introduced loan systems, and enforced a rice tax increase (jōmai).
Akihiro encouraged {{ill|Confucianism|en|Confucianism|qid=Q9581|lt=Confucianism|1=Confucianism}} within his domain. In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|qid=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 8 (1788), he invited {{ill|Akutagawa Motodō|ja|芥川元澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akutagawa Motodō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from Kyoto as a Confucian scholar to lecture his retainers. This marked the foundation of the domain's academy, {{ill|Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|ja|進徳館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintokukan (Sabae Domain)|1=Q20037568|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20037568}}. Akihiro himself was said to excel in mounted archery.
Akihiro passed away in {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|qid=Q11042|lt=Bunka|1=Culture}} 8 (1812) on the 21st day of the 12th month at the age of 42. He was succeeded by his eldest son, {{ill|Manabe Akinobu|ja|間部詮允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabe Akinobu|1=Manabe Akisane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11655468}}. His posthumous Buddhist name was Shinrei-in (真霊院), and he is buried at {{ill|Mankeiji Temple|ja|万慶寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mankeiji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 萬慶寺|1=Bankei-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620477}} in {{ill|Sabae, Fukui|en|Sabae, Fukui|qid=Q847598|lt=Sabae|1=Sabae, Fukui}}, Fukui Prefecture.
